---
title: CLI
---

# Basic usage

Render a `.qmd` file to HTML (the default format):

```bash
calepin document.qmd
```

This produces `document.html` in the same directory.


# Output formats

*Calepin* supports four output formats: `html`, `latex`, `typst`, and `markdown`.

```bash
calepin document.qmd --format latex    # → document.tex
calepin document.qmd --format typst    # → document.typ
calepin document.qmd --format markdown # → document.md
```

You can also set the format in the YAML front matter:

```yaml
---
format: latex
---
```

Or let *Calepin* infer it from the output file extension with `-o`:

```bash
calepin document.qmd -o paper.tex
```


# PDF compilation

The `--compile` flag renders the document and then compiles the output to PDF. It uses [tectonic](https://tectonic-typesetting.github.io/) for LaTeX and [typst](https://typst.app/) for Typst.

```bash
calepin document.qmd --format latex --compile  # → document.pdf (via tectonic)
calepin document.qmd --format typst --compile  # → document.pdf (via typst)
```


# Live preview

The `--preview` flag starts a local server, opens your browser, and live-reloads whenever you save the file:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --preview
```

This serves the rendered HTML at `http://localhost:3456` by default. Use `--port` to change the port:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --preview --port 8080
```

For LaTeX and Typst, preview compiles to PDF and reloads the PDF viewer on each save.

Press `Ctrl+C` to stop the preview server.


# Metadata overrides

The `--set` flag overrides YAML front matter values from the command line. This is useful for toggling options without editing the file:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --set toc=true
calepin document.qmd --set title="Draft" toc=false number-sections=true
```

Multiple key-value pairs can follow a single `--set`:

```bash
calepin document.qmd --set bibliography=refs.bib fig-width=6
```


# Quiet mode

Suppress progress messages:

```bash
calepin document.qmd -q
```


# Examples

```bash
# Render to HTML with table of contents
calepin paper.qmd --set toc=true

# Render to PDF via Typst, overriding the title
calepin paper.qmd --format typst --compile --set title="Final Version"

# Live preview while writing
calepin paper.qmd --preview
```
